pages.title: Reference Index — Leak Classes, Plug Classes & Concepts
pages.description: Governed index of Funnelplugs leak classes, plug classes, and conceptual papers. Central hub for structural reference material.

hero.title: Reference index
hero.lead: Structured entry points into leak taxonomy, plug taxonomy, and conceptual foundations used across the sovereign system.

summary.title: How to use this index
summary.body: Start from a leak class to understand failure mode language, or from a plug class to understand intervention language. Concept pages explain governing ideas that cut across both taxonomies.

cards.title: Trust Collapse
cards.body: Structural weakness when decision-stage confidence is under-supported by proof, reassurance, and legitimacy.

cards.title: Flow Break
cards.body: Continuity loss between stages: interruption, disorientation, or movement failure before completion.

cards.title: Friction Overload
cards.body: Avoidable effort, complexity, or burden suppresses movement and completion.

cards.title: Decision Ambiguity
cards.body: Insufficient clarity on value, choice, or next step at the moment a decision is required.

cards.title: Recovery Absence
cards.body: Missing recovery infrastructure after hesitation, interruption, or abandonment.

cards.title: Message-Path Misalignment
cards.body: Promise, context, and on-path action logic diverge across the journey.

cards.title: Trust Infrastructure
cards.body: Intervention layers that restore confidence, legitimacy, reassurance, and proof at decision-critical moments.

cards.title: Flow Continuity Layer
cards.body: Preserves movement, reduces transition failure, and maintains continuity from entry to action.

cards.title: Friction Reduction Layer
cards.body: Reduces avoidable effort and completion drag without degrading structural clarity.

cards.title: Decision Guidance Layer
cards.body: Improves interpretability, value clarity, and next-step certainty at choice points.

cards.title: Recovery Systems Layer
cards.body: Builds continuation logic after hesitation, interruption, or abandonment.

cards.title: Alignment Reinforcement Layer
cards.body: Restores continuity between promise, context, and on-path action logic.

cards.title: Structural Revenue Leakage
cards.body: Revenue loss interpreted as missing structural layers in the commercial path rather than traffic shortage alone.

cards.title: Commercial Path Continuity
cards.body: The requirement that intent can move through stages without unjustified interruption or discontinuity.

cards.title: Decision-Stage Integrity
cards.body: The path must support confident interpretation whenever a commitment or meaningful choice is required.

cards.title: Intervention Architecture
cards.body: The ordered set of structural plugs required to restore integrity after diagnosis.

cards.title: Diagnostic Signal Layers
cards.body: How observable behavior and structured questions map into leak and plug classes.

cards.title: Abandonment & Recovery Logic
cards.body: Why interruption should trigger continuation design instead of treating users as permanently lost.

cards.title: Canonical URL Governance
cards.body: Self-referencing canonical URLs keep indexing signals coherent for static sovereign output.

cards.title: Integrity Band Interpretation
cards.body: How scored integrity bands summarize overall path strength without pretending precision is prophecy.

cards.title: Dimensional Weakness Mapping
cards.body: Mapping friction, trust, clarity, recovery, and related dimensions to structural diagnoses.

cards.title: Plug Class Prioritization
cards.body: Choosing primary and secondary plug classes based on structural dependency and diagnosed leaks.

cards.title: Leak-to-Plug Mapping
cards.body: How each leak class connects to recommended plug classes inside the governed taxonomies.

pages.title: Trust Collapse — Revenue Leak Class
pages.description: Structural weakness when decision-stage confidence is under-supported by proof, reassurance, and legitimacy.

hero.title: Trust Collapse
hero.lead: Confidence fails at the commitment boundary even when basic interest exists.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: This leak class describes trust architecture that is too weak for the decision the path is asking the user to make.

sections.title: Structural read
sections.body: Trust Collapse means the path reaches a decision point without enough confidence infrastructure to sustain commitment.

sections.title: Operational signal
sections.body: Late-stage abandonment with lingering interest often traces here before creative or traffic fixes.

pages.title: Flow Break — Revenue Leak Class
pages.description: Continuity loss between stages: interruption, disorientation, or movement failure before completion.

hero.title: Flow Break
hero.lead: Momentum is lost across transitions even when individual screens look acceptable.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Flow Break isolates structural discontinuity rather than blaming isolated UX polish.

sections.title: Structural read
sections.body: The visitor cannot move through the path with stable continuity.

sections.title: Operational signal
sections.body: Drop-off clusters around transitions, steps, or hand-offs rather than the offer itself.

pages.title: Friction Overload — Revenue Leak Class
pages.description: Avoidable effort, complexity, or burden suppresses movement and completion.

hero.title: Friction Overload
hero.lead: The system demands more operational or cognitive work than the user should need to continue.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Friction Overload separates burden created by the path from simple offer rejection.

sections.title: Structural read
sections.body: Process heaviness erodes intent that would otherwise continue.

sections.title: Operational signal
sections.body: Completion falls during action-heavy sequences despite continued relevance.

pages.title: Decision Ambiguity — Revenue Leak Class
pages.description: Insufficient clarity on value, choice, or next step at the moment a decision is required.

hero.title: Decision Ambiguity
hero.lead: The user cannot interpret the commitment confidently even when curiosity remains.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: This class targets interpretive weakness at choice points, not generic copy weakness everywhere.

sections.title: Structural read
sections.body: The path under-explains what is being chosen and what happens next.

sections.title: Operational signal
sections.body: Hesitation without strong objection; users pause instead of advancing.

pages.title: Recovery Absence — Revenue Leak Class
pages.description: Missing recovery infrastructure after hesitation, interruption, or abandonment.

hero.title: Recovery Absence
hero.lead: Interrupted intent is treated as terminal instead of recoverable.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Recovery Absence highlights absent continuation logic, not a single email tactic.

sections.title: Structural read
sections.body: The path surrenders momentum once the primary flow is broken.

sections.title: Operational signal
sections.body: Strong first-pass interest but weak return paths after pause or exit.

pages.title: Message-Path Misalignment — Revenue Leak Class
pages.description: Promise, context, and on-path action logic diverge across the journey.

hero.title: Message-Path Misalignment
hero.lead: Entry intent is not structurally honored by what the path asks next.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Misalignment contaminates the journey before local optimizations can compensate.

sections.title: Structural read
sections.body: Expectation created upstream is not what the path continues to serve.

sections.title: Operational signal
sections.body: Engagement starts then decays after arrival despite competent isolated pages.

pages.title: Trust Infrastructure — Plug Class
pages.description: Intervention layers that restore confidence, legitimacy, reassurance, and proof at decision-critical moments.

hero.title: Trust Infrastructure
hero.lead: Reinforce credibility and safety where commitment is required.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Trust Infrastructure is deployed where the path asks for commitment before confidence is structurally supported.

sections.title: Purpose
sections.body: Reduce trust-based hesitation at conversion boundaries.

sections.title: Fit
sections.body: Often pairs with Trust Collapse and late-stage hesitation patterns.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Friction reduction must preserve interpretive integrity; it is not blind simplification.

sections.title: Purpose

sections.title: Fit

pages.title: Structural Revenue Leakage — Funnelplugs
pages.description: Revenue loss interpreted as missing structural layers in the commercial path rather than traffic shortage alone.

hero.title: Structural revenue leakage
hero.lead: Leakage is read as incompleteness in the journey architecture, not only campaign volume.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: This concept anchors how Funnelplugs interprets underperformance without collapsing it into generic marketing failure.

sections.title: Definition
sections.body: Structural leakage appears when required intervention layers are absent or weak at critical boundaries.

sections.title: Implication
sections.body: Fixes prioritize architecture before incremental creative tweaks.

pages.title: Commercial Path Continuity — Funnelplugs
pages.description: The requirement that intent can move through stages without unjustified interruption or discontinuity.

hero.title: Commercial path continuity
hero.lead: Continuity means stable progression logic, not merely fast pages.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Continuity is one of the governing dimensions behind Flow Break and Flow Continuity interventions.

sections.title: Definition
sections.body: Continuity is present when transitions preserve momentum and orientation.

sections.title: Implication
sections.body: Broken hand-offs are treated as structural defects, not cosmetic issues.

pages.title: Decision-Stage Integrity — Funnelplugs
pages.description: The path must support confident interpretation whenever a commitment or meaningful choice is required.

hero.title: Decision-stage integrity
hero.lead: Integrity at the decision boundary requires clarity and trust working together.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: This concept links Decision Ambiguity to governed guidance rather than louder persuasion.

sections.title: Definition
sections.body: Decision integrity fails when users must guess value, risk, or next steps.

sections.title: Implication
sections.body: Clarify commitment logic before scaling traffic or incentives.

pages.title: Intervention Architecture — Funnelplugs
pages.description: The ordered set of structural plugs required to restore integrity after diagnosis.

hero.title: Intervention architecture
hero.lead: Interventions are classes of restoration, not random tool additions.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Architecture thinking keeps plug selection aligned with leak class and dependency order.

sections.title: Definition
sections.body: An intervention architecture specifies what must be fixed first and why.

sections.title: Implication
sections.body: Prevents stacking tactics that ignore the primary structural blocker.

hero.lead: Signals are interpreted through governed rules, not anecdotal opinion.

summary.title: Scope

sections.title: Definition
sections.body: Signals aggregate into interpretable classes with explicit methodology.

sections.title: Implication
sections.body: Diagnostics remain auditable and repeatable across pages and teams.

pages.title: Abandonment & Recovery Logic — Funnelplugs
pages.description: Why interruption should trigger continuation design instead of treating users as permanently lost.

hero.title: Abandonment and recovery logic
hero.lead: Recovery converts salvageable demand into structured re-entry.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Grounds the Recovery Absence leak and Recovery Systems plug in operational terms.

sections.title: Definition
sections.body: Recovery logic defines how intent is invited back without breaking trust.

sections.title: Implication
sections.body: Build re-entry paths proportional to the diagnosed interruption pattern.

pages.title: Canonical URL Governance — Funnelplugs
pages.description: Self-referencing canonical URLs keep indexing signals coherent for static sovereign output.

hero.title: Canonical URL governance
hero.lead: Each indexable page should declare one HTTPS canonical under the site root.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Governance reduces duplicate signals and clarifies which URL represents each document.

sections.title: Definition
sections.body: Canonical policy ties each HTML file to a single preferred URL.

sections.title: Implication
sections.body: Sitemap entries should match canonical declarations on the live pages.

pages.title: Integrity Band Interpretation — Funnelplugs
pages.description: How scored integrity bands summarize overall path strength without pretending precision is prophecy.

hero.title: Integrity band interpretation
hero.lead: Bands compress multidimensional weakness into prioritized action framing.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Connects engine scoring outputs to strategic sequencing.

sections.title: Definition
sections.body: Bands group similar risk postures for communication and prioritization.

sections.title: Implication
sections.body: Use bands to order plug work, not to imply guaranteed revenue outcomes.

hero.title: Dimensional weakness mapping
hero.lead: Dimensions reveal where the path fails even when funnels look complete.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Supports cross-checking between leak classes and standard dimensions.

sections.title: Definition
sections.body: Each dimension highlights a distinct failure mode in the journey.

sections.title: Implication
sections.body: Multiple weak dimensions may require sequenced interventions.

pages.title: Plug Class Prioritization — Funnelplugs
pages.description: Choosing primary and secondary plug classes based on structural dependency and diagnosed leaks.

hero.title: Plug class prioritization
hero.lead: Order interventions by dependency, not by ease of installation.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: Keeps remediation aligned with the plug taxonomy decision framework.

sections.title: Definition
sections.body: Primary plug class tracks the dominant structural blocker.

sections.title: Implication
sections.body: Secondary plugs follow when materially adjacent and operationally justified.

pages.title: Leak-to-Plug Mapping — Funnelplugs
pages.description: How each leak class connects to recommended plug classes inside the governed taxonomies.

hero.title: Leak-to-plug mapping
hero.lead: Mapping is explicit so recommendations stay interpretable.

summary.title: Scope
summary.body: This concept bridges diagnostic language and intervention language without collapsing them.

sections.title: Definition
sections.body: Each leak class points to plug classes that restore the missing layer.

sections.title: Implication
sections.body: Avoid recommending plugs that ignore the diagnosed primary leak.
